column1: Halakha (Jewish law) is the overarching framework for all these practices, codified in sources like the Mishnah, Talmud, Shulchan Aruch, and later responsa6.
Hasidic and Kabbalistic customs often add mystical or communal dimensions to standard practices, such as ecstatic prayer, storytelling, and specific dress7.
Many practices are shared across all denominations but may be interpreted or emphasized differently (e.g., role of women, frequency, language of prayer)58.
Torah (Pentateuch): Genesis, Exodus, Leviticus, Numbers, Deuteronomy
Shulchan Aruch: Standard code of Jewish law
Hasidic tradition: Oral and written teachings of Hasidic masters

For more detailed references, consult the Shulchan Aruch, Mishnah, Talmud, and the Zohar for Kabbalistic rituals675. For contemporary customs and denominational variations, see guides to Jewish practice and ethnographic studies148.
